articles.title: Four in five landlords unprepared for Renters’ Right Act
articles.summary: Four in five landlords (82%) are concerned about the impact of the Renters’ Rights Act, with one in two (44%) very concerned. Meanwhile, three-quarters (75%) of landlords say regulation has made them less likely to grow their portfolios, and half (49%) are planning to sell or reduce their stake within two months.
articles.ai_summary:
- 82% of landlords are worried about the impacts of the Renters' Rights Act, with 44% expressing significant concern.  
- 75% feel regulatory changes discourage portfolio growth, prompting nearly half (49%) to consider selling or downsizing within two months.

articles.title: Energy efficiency and EPC ratings become higher focus for tenants
articles.summary: Landlords must consider how to improve EPC ratings to attract tenants, even ahead of the new requirement to upgrade rental properties to a C rating by 2030. It comes as energy efficiency of properties has gained even greater focus as tenants look to save money after the energy price hikes caused by the Iran War.
articles.ai_summary:
- Landlords are urged to enhance EPC ratings to attract tenants, especially with the looming requirement for a C rating by 2030.  
- Rising energy costs due to the Iran War have intensified tenants' focus on energy-efficient properties to save on expenses.

articles.title: Planning reform fails to inspire confidence – Landbay
articles.summary: Fewer than a quarter of the UK’s landlords feel planning reform will positively impact their business.
articles.ai_summary:
- Less than 25% of UK landlords believe planning reforms will benefit their businesses.  
- Current sentiment among landlords indicates a lack of confidence in proposed planning changes.

articles.title: Landlords warned a hidden leak could cost thousands
articles.summary: With more than half (58%) of hidden leaks originating outside a property, landlords must remember to focus on the maintenance of pipework outside as well as inside the property, leak detection specialist Aspect has warned.
articles.ai_summary:
- Over 58% of hidden leaks in UK properties originate from outside, urging landlords to prioritize external pipework maintenance.  
- Neglecting external pipes could lead to costly repairs, as warned by leak detection expert Aspect.

articles.title: As RRA deadline looms, 5 top tips for landlords
articles.summary: From 1 May 2026, the Renters’ Rights Act will implement some of the biggest changes to the Private Rented Sector in England and Wales. The Act will aim to balance landlord and tenant rights and will impose new requirements and regulatory oversight on landlords.
articles.ai_summary:
- The Renters' Rights Act, effective May 1, 2026, will introduce significant changes to the Private Rented Sector in England and Wales, enhancing tenant rights and imposing new regulations on landlords.  
- Landlords are advised to prepare by following five key tips to navigate the upcoming legal landscape and ensure compliance with the new Act.

articles.title: 84% of landlords not ready for RRA – survey
articles.summary: More than eight in ten (84%) of landlords are unprepared for the Renters’ Rights Act go-live on May 1, with 58% at high risk of non-compliance according to new figures.
articles.ai_summary:
- **Over 80% of landlords unprepared:** A staggering 84% of landlords are not ready for the Renters’ Rights Act, set to take effect on May 1.  
- **High risk of non-compliance:** 58% of these landlords face a significant risk of violating the new regulations.

articles.title: Expansion no longer default response to rising demand as rental growth slows
articles.summary: Landlords are restructuring portfolios and financing in response to changing rental demand, rather than focusing on expansion, with the ONS announcement of a slowing in rental growth showing the need to ensure portfolios are right.
articles.ai_summary:
- Landlords are shifting strategies from expansion to restructuring portfolios and financing as rental growth slows, highlighting the need for alignment with current market demands.
- The latest ONS data reveals a deceleration in rental growth, prompting a reevaluation of property investments among landlords.

articles.title: Landlords blamed for pushing first-time and local buyers out
articles.summary: Landlords and investors have been blamed for pushing first-time buyers and local buyers out of the market, according to a new survey.
articles.ai_summary:
- A recent survey indicates landlords and investors are driving first-time and local buyers out of the property market.  
- Rising competition from these buyers is creating barriers for those looking to purchase their first home.

articles.title: Next government must stem landlord flow
articles.summary: Scotland's landlords have called for the next Scottish Government to formally recognise the private rented sector's (PRS) importance with a dedicated strategy.
articles.ai_summary:
- Scottish landlords urge the next government to acknowledge the significance of the private rented sector (PRS) with a focused strategy.  
- A call for action highlights the need to halt the exodus of landlords and stabilize the rental market in Scotland.

articles.title: Landlords beware of registration requirement for tax advisor
articles.summary: Conveyancing solicitors and estate agency payroll teams are among those who could fall foul of new mandatory HMRC tax advisor registration requirements from May, facing penalties of up to £10,000.
articles.ai_summary:
- From May, HMRC mandates that tax advisors, including conveyancing solicitors and estate agency payroll teams, must register to avoid facing penalties up to £10,000.
- Landlords need to stay informed about these new registration requirements to ensure compliance and avoid costly fines.

articles.title: Licensing doesn’t lead to higher rents – council’s claim
articles.summary: The council is expecting landlords to pay £694 per property without recovering the cost
articles.ai_summary:
- Council asserts that implementing licensing for landlords will not result in higher rental prices for tenants.  
- Landlords face a £694 cost per property for licensing, which the council anticipates will not be recouped.

articles.title: Labour makes it clear – no rent controls
articles.summary: The pledge was made by Matthew Pennycook
articles.ai_summary:
- Labour Party's Matthew Pennycook confirms that rent controls will not be implemented under their policy framework.
- The stance emphasizes a commitment to addressing housing issues without resorting to rent regulation.

articles.title: Authority wants to stop “HMO landlord free for all”
articles.summary: The council is also drawing up detailed HMO guidelines
articles.ai_summary:
- The council is taking action to regulate the growing trend of Houses in Multiple Occupation (HMOs) to prevent an unchecked landlord situation.  
- New detailed guidelines for HMOs are being developed to ensure better oversight and management in the housing sector.

articles.title: Tenants tutored on maximising Renters Rights Act powers
articles.summary: The week-long campaign begins on Monday
articles.ai_summary:
- Tenants are set to receive guidance on leveraging their rights under the Renters Rights Act through a new week-long campaign starting Monday.
- The initiative aims to empower renters by educating them on their legal entitlements and protections within the rental market.

articles.title: Renters Rights Act provoking spike in S21 evictions
articles.summary: This is exactly what Paul Shamplina predicted
articles.ai_summary:
- The Renters Rights Act is leading to a surge in Section 21 evictions, validating Paul Shamplina's predictions.  
- Landlords are increasingly utilizing legal avenues to reclaim properties as new regulations come into effect.

articles.title: Rents rising ahead of Renters Rights Act – new data
articles.summary: The data comes from the lettings agency Hamptons
articles.ai_summary:
- Rents are increasing as landlords prepare for the upcoming Renters Rights Act, according to new data from Hamptons.
- The trend reflects a proactive adjustment in the rental market ahead of regulatory changes aimed at protecting tenants.

articles.title: Renters Rights Act will kill off the hobbyist landlord – opinion
articles.summary: We're just 10 days away from the Renters Rights Act becoming law
articles.ai_summary:
- The imminent implementation of the Renters Rights Act is poised to eliminate the viability of hobbyist landlords in the UK property market.
- Critics argue that the new legislation could significantly impact the ability of casual landlords to manage rental properties effectively.

articles.title: Gen Z entrepreneurs turn to property investment
articles.summary: Despite the red tape and extra tax poured on property investors in recent years.
articles.ai_summary:
- Gen Z entrepreneurs are increasingly venturing into property investment, undeterred by recent regulatory hurdles and tax challenges.  
- This growing trend highlights a shift among younger generations seeking alternative avenues for wealth generation in a changing economic landscape.

articles.title: Tough market for sellers, claims house buying group
articles.summary:
Sellers forced to cut asking prices, endure failed sales or withdraw from the market, reveals House Buyer Bureau’s Chris Hodgkinson.
The post Tough market for sellers, claims house buying group appeared first on The Negotiator.
articles.ai_summary:
- Sellers are facing challenges as they cut asking prices or withdraw from the market due to increased failed sales, according to House Buyer Bureau's Chris Hodgkinson.  
- The current market conditions indicate a tough environment for sellers, highlighting ongoing struggles in property transactions.

articles.title: HMRC ‘ramps up’ scrutiny of property valuations for Inheritance Tax
articles.summary:
The number of cases referred to the Valuation Office Agency (VOA) is increasing, boosting Inheritance Tax receipts.
The post HMRC ‘ramps up’ scrutiny of property valuations for Inheritance Tax appeared first on The Negotiator.
articles.ai_summary:
- HMRC intensifies scrutiny of property valuations, leading to a surge in referrals to the Valuation Office Agency (VOA).  
- This heightened oversight is resulting in increased Inheritance Tax receipts for the UK government.

articles.title: Sleeping tenant caught on camera in £135k property listing photos
articles.summary:
The tenant is seen asleep in bed in the property listing photographs and video for a terrace property for sale in Penrith, Cumbria.
The post Sleeping tenant caught on camera in £135k property listing photos appeared first on The Negotiator.
articles.ai_summary:
- A £135k terrace property in Penrith, Cumbria, features a surprising twist in its listing: a sleeping tenant appears in the showcased photos and video.
- The unusual oversight has drawn attention to the property's marketing practices in the competitive real estate market.

articles.title: Referral fees are a ‘hidden tax’ on homebuyers
articles.summary:
Referral fees have operated as an “unseen surcharge’ on homebuyers, according to the Conveyancing Task Force.
The post Referral fees are a ‘hidden tax’ on homebuyers appeared first on The Negotiator.
articles.ai_summary:
- Referral fees are dubbed a "hidden tax" on homebuyers, adding unseen surcharges to the cost of purchasing property, as revealed by the Conveyancing Task Force.  
- The issue highlights the need for greater transparency in the property market to protect buyers from unexpected costs.

articles.title: Lomond expands operation north of the border
articles.summary:
David Alexander, of Lomond, says Clyde Property Group will run its portfolio in the West of Scotland.
The post Lomond expands operation north of the border appeared first on The Negotiator.
articles.ai_summary:
- Lomond is expanding its operations into Scotland, with Clyde Property Group managing its property portfolio in the West.
- This strategic move signals Lomond's growth and commitment to strengthening its presence in the Scottish real estate market.

articles.title: Foxtons’ sales revenue plummets 35%
articles.summary:
Geopolitical headwinds have meant that new buyer activity was lower than expected, says Foxtons’ CEO Guy Gittins.
The post Foxtons’ sales revenue plummets 35% appeared first on The Negotiator.
articles.ai_summary:
- Foxtons reports a staggering 35% drop in sales revenue, attributing the decline to geopolitical challenges.  
- CEO Guy Gittins reveals that new buyer activity has fallen short of expectations due to these external pressures.

articles.title: Inflation rises as Middle East conflict drives up prices
articles.summary:
Price rises have hit fuel and transport hardest but are expected to soon spread to the rest of the economy.
The post Inflation rises as Middle East conflict drives up prices appeared first on The Negotiator.
articles.ai_summary:
- Inflation is surging, with fuel and transport prices experiencing the most significant increases due to the Middle East conflict.
- Experts anticipate that these rising costs will soon ripple through other sectors of the economy.

articles.title: Nine out of ten homes don’t reach the open sales market
articles.summary:
The reality is that many new homes being built will never reach the market, says Riccardo Iannucci-Dawson, Chief Executive of Alto.
The post Nine out of ten homes don’t reach the open sales market appeared first on The Negotiator.
articles.ai_summary:
- **Market Access Challenge:** 90% of newly constructed homes are never listed on the open sales market, highlighting a significant disparity in property availability, according to Riccardo Iannucci-Dawson, CEO of Alto.  
- **Implications for Buyers:** This situation underscores challenges for prospective buyers seeking new homes, as many properties remain off-market and inaccessible.
